semanticCore: Swallowing at one gulp, taking food into the mouth, morsel of food, or block/obstruct

keyDerivations.meaning: To swallow at one gulp, take quickly into the mouth, or engulf

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَالْتَقَمَهُ الْحُوتُ وَهُوَ مُلِيمٌ (37:142): So the fish/whale swallowed him (Jonah) while he was blameworthy.
discardedNoise: Luqmah (bite-size food morsel) - common food noun.
Luqman (proper name) - unrelated prophet/wise man name.
summaryEn: The root means taking food into the mouth or swallowing whole (Laqm), describing the giant whale swallowing Jonah as part of his spiritual test.
summaryAr: يشير هذا الجذر إلى بلع الشيء بلقمة واحدة وأخذه بسرعة في الفم (لقم)، واصفاً التقاط الحوت ليونس وابتلاعه بالكامل دون خدشه.
rawText: أَلْقَمَ 4 He put morsels into the mouth of [such a one]. See 2. === أَلْقَمْتُهُ الحَجَرَ † I silenced him in an altercation. (Msb.) === أَلْقَمَهُ البِرْطِيلَ; see the latter word. إِلْتَقَمَ 8 see 1. === اِلْتَقَمَ فَاهَا فِى التَّقْبِيلِ [He took her mouth within his lips in kissing]. (S in art. كعم.) Hence the verb signifies † It embraced, or clasped, a thing: see an instance, voce مِحْصَنٌ. تَلَقَّمَ اللُّقْمَهَ 5 He swallowed the gobbet, or morsel, or mouthful, in a leisurely manner. (S, TA.) لَقِمَ لُقْمَةً 1 , [aor. ﹷ,] inf. n. لَقْمٌ, (JK, MS,) [He gobbled a gobbet, or morsel, or mouthful, or] he swallowed the gobbet; and so 🡻اِلْتَقَمَهَا. (S.) لَقِمَهُ, aor. ﹷ, (Msb, K, &c.,) inf. n. لَقْمٌ, (JK, MA, K, MS, JM,) or لَقَمٌ (Msb, [app. a mistake,]) He gobbled it; i. e., ate it quickly, (Msb, K, TA,) as also 🡻اِلْتَقَمَهُ, (Msb, [see above,]) and hastily; drew it with his mouth, and ate it quickly. (TA) === لَقِمَ بِيَدِهِ He put morsels into his mouth (i. e., his own mouth) with his hand. See an ex. voce رَقَعَ. This seems to be the primary signification. لَقَّمَهُ الطَّعَامَ 2 and 🡻أَلْقَمَهُ [He fed with the food by the mouthful; put it into his mouth by the mouthful]: (Msb:) or لَقَّمَهُ إِيَّاهُ and 🡻أَلْقَمَهُ إِيَّاهُ he put a mouthful [of it] into his mouth. (TA.) And لَقَّمَهُ and 🡻أَلْقَمَهُ [He fed him by the mouthful; put mouthfuls into his mouth]. (S.) لُقَيْمَاتُ القَاضِى Same as لُقَمَاتُ القاضى. لُقْمَةٌ A morsel, gobbet, or mouthful: what is [swallowed, or] eaten quickly, at once, of bread [&c.]; like as جُرْعَةٌ signifies what is swallowed at once in drinking: (Msb:) or what is prepared to be [swallowed, or] eaten quickly [at once]. (K, TA.) === لُقْمَةٌ القَاضِى, and لُقْمَةُ الخَلِيفَةِ: see زُمَاوَرْدٌ, art. ورد; and مُيَسَّرٌ.
